card_description: Mr. Kelly is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick identifies "Astraea/Virgo" (Justice) as the entity that healed his blood pressure and promised judgment by fire for the despoilers of Earth (Vietnam)."

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies "Astraea/Virgo" (Justice) as the entity that healed his blood pressure and promised judgment by fire for the despoilers of Earth (Vietnam). He reinterprets reality as a "Two-Player Gam
linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_49.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick bridges his personal experience with the "regression of things" in *Ubik*, proposing that the universe is a "mutually hallucinated" world where humans are "half-alive" (in cold-pac) being roused
linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_48.txt
